title: Clear Floor Obstruction Or Dropped Parcel Using E-STOP Zone Isolation And Reset
summary: Use the documented troubleshooting recovery sequence to isolate the affected E-stop zone, remove a dropped parcel or debris obstruction from the floor, reset the E-STOP condition, and restore system operation.
when_to_use: Use when there is an obstruction or parcel on the floor and the documented troubleshooting sequence calls for referencing the E-stop zone map, stopping the appropriate E-stop zone, removing the parcel or debris, pulling the E-STOP, and pressing the blue RESET button.
not_for: Do not use for recovery conditions beyond the documented obstruction/parcel on floor reset sequence.
Do not proceed beyond the documented steps if the system does not resume operation after pressing the blue RESET button.

responsibilities: Identify the appropriate E-stop zone using the E-stop zone map.
Stop the appropriate E-stop zone using E-STOP.
Remove the parcel or debris from the floor.
Pull the E-STOP and press the blue RESET button.
Verify that the system resumes operation.

safety_notes: Use the E-stop zone map to identify the appropriate zone before pressing E-STOP.
Only the documented zone isolation and reset sequence is provided by the source.
If the system does not resume operation after the blue RESET button is pressed, stop and escalate.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the E-stop zone map
Access to the E-STOP control for the appropriate zone
Access to the blue RESET button
Physical access to remove the parcel or debris

access_required: Access to the affected floor area
Access to the appropriate E-STOP control
Access to the blue RESET button
system_preconditions: An obstruction, dropped parcel, or debris is present on the floor.
The appropriate E-stop zone can be identified from the E-stop zone map.

visual_references.description: Related fault recovery screen artifact attached from the packet for reset-oriented recovery context; candidate requested image support for the reset step.
System screen artifact tied to ESTOP clearing and RESET usage in nearby manual content.

steps.title: Identify the affected E-stop zone
steps.purpose: Ensure the correct zone is isolated before attempting obstruction removal.
steps.instruction: Reference the E-stop zone map to identify the appropriate E-stop zone for the obstruction.
steps.expected_result: The correct E-stop zone for the floor obstruction is identified.
steps.healthy_condition: The operator can clearly determine which E-stop zone corresponds to the obstruction location.
steps.failure_condition: The appropriate E-stop zone cannot be confidently identified from the available map or reference.
stop_or_escalate_if: The appropriate E-stop zone cannot be identified.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Obstruction/parcel on floor... 1. Reference E-stop zone map. Press E-STOP to stop the appropriate E-stop zone.

steps.title: Stop the appropriate E-stop zone
steps.purpose: Safely isolate the affected zone before removing the obstruction.
steps.instruction: Press E-STOP to stop the appropriate E-stop zone.
steps.expected_result: The appropriate E-stop zone is stopped.
steps.healthy_condition: The affected zone is stopped and the obstruction area is isolated for removal.
steps.failure_condition: The zone does not stop as expected or the wrong zone appears to be affected.

actions.label: Press E-STOP
actions.exact_action: Press E-STOP to stop the appropriate E-stop zone.

stop_or_escalate_if: The zone does not stop as expected.
The wrong zone appears to have been selected.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Obstruction/parcel on floor... 1. Reference E-stop zone map. Press E-STOP to stop the appropriate E-stop zone.

steps.title: Remove the parcel or debris
steps.purpose: Clear the floor obstruction that caused the recovery condition.
steps.instruction: Remove the parcel or debris from the floor.
steps.expected_result: The obstruction is physically removed from the floor.
steps.healthy_condition: The floor area is clear of the dropped parcel or debris.
steps.failure_condition: The parcel or debris cannot be removed or the obstruction condition remains present.
stop_or_escalate_if: The obstruction cannot be safely removed.
Debris or parcel material remains on the floor after removal attempt.

steps.title: Pull the E-STOP
steps.purpose: Clear the E-STOP condition as part of the documented reset sequence.
steps.expected_result: The E-STOP is pulled out and ready for reset completion.
steps.healthy_condition: The E-STOP condition is cleared to the extent required for the next reset step.
steps.failure_condition: The E-STOP cannot be pulled or the E-STOP condition does not clear.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Reference the ESTOP clearing context associated with pulling out the ESTOP before pressing RESET.

stop_or_escalate_if: The E-STOP cannot be reset to the pulled-out state.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: At the operator station control panel, if the ESTOP is flashing, pull out the ESTOP and press RESET on the HMI screen to clear any faults.

steps.title: Press the blue RESET button
steps.purpose: Complete the documented recovery reset sequence so the system can return to operation.
steps.expected_result: The reset is accepted and the system proceeds toward resumed operation.
steps.healthy_condition: The reset action clears the fault condition associated with the obstruction recovery sequence.
steps.failure_condition: The reset does not clear the condition or the system does not proceed toward resumed operation.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Candidate-requested image support for the reset step; use as related fault recovery visual context from the packet.
RESET-related HMI context associated with clearing faults after the ESTOP is pulled out.

stop_or_escalate_if: The system does not resume operation after pressing the blue RESET button.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: At the operator station control panel, if the ESTOP is flashing, pull out the ESTOP and press RESET on the HMI screen to clear any faults.

steps.title: Verify system resumes operation
steps.purpose: Confirm that the documented recovery sequence successfully restored operation.
steps.instruction: Verify that the system resumes operation.
steps.healthy_condition: Normal system operation resumes after the reset sequence.
steps.failure_condition: The system does not resume operation after the reset sequence.
stop_or_escalate_if: The system does not resume operation after pressing the blue RESET button.

success_criteria: The parcel or debris is removed from the floor.
The E-stop zone is reset using the documented pull E-STOP and blue RESET sequence.
healthy_conditions: The correct E-stop zone is identified and stopped.
The floor obstruction is fully removed.
The E-STOP can be pulled out and the RESET action can be performed.
The system resumes operation after reset.
failure_conditions: The appropriate E-stop zone cannot be identified.
The zone does not stop as expected.
The E-STOP cannot be pulled out or reset sequence cannot be completed.
The system does not resume operation after pressing the blue RESET button.
escalation_guidance: Stop and escalate if the system does not resume operation after pressing the blue RESET button.
The source does not provide additional recovery steps beyond the reset sequence.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Related fault recovery screen artifact included by the candidate for reset-step image support.
RESET-related HMI context for clearing faults after pulling out ESTOP.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: Obstruction/parcel on floor... 1. Reference E-stop zone map. Press E-STOP to stop the appropriate E-stop zone. 2. Remove the parcel/debris. 3. Pull E-STOP. 4. Press the blue RESET button. 5. The system will resume operation.

missing_details: The source does not specify exact physical location or format of the E-stop zone map.
The source does not specify exact location of the blue RESET button.
The source does not provide additional troubleshooting or alternate recovery steps if reset fails.
The source does not provide a time estimate for completing this procedure.
The packet does not include a dedicated artifact of the E-stop zone map.
